title: Longman rubber order 2026-05-08
release_position: Longman quote pack uses square BM-ISO-SM/BM-ISO-LG body pads, FS-OVAL pads, first-article bump stops, and hold rows for FS-STRIP-L/R plus possible full-width liners.
notes: Steel cup/seat washers, sleeves, shims, and bolts are separate hardware. FS-STRIP-L/R and full-width liners remain measurement holds until physical carrier/location evidence proves them.

title: Suspension wood cribbing Rev A
release_position: Backup drawing pack for the suspension cribbing buy: 8 rectangular blocks plus 4 wedge chocks.
notes: Shop ask is owned by suspension_upgrade through docs/suspension-wood-cribbing-merchant-spec.md; this package is the drawing backup. Supplemental cribbing/chocks only; not a substitute for rated stands.

title: Electrical modules Rev A
release_position: Earlier combined under-bonnet relay/power module route with relay tray, power module box, and rear insulator.
notes: Keep as reference/provisional unless the combined module route is deliberately reopened.

title: MIDI 5-way plate mount Rev C
release_position: Current recommended open MIDI holder mount: 190 x 150 x 3.0 mm aluminium plate plus 140 x 85 x 5.0 mm non-conductive holder subplate.
notes: Required definition is the Rev C PDF/DXF/SVG set in millimeters; external plastic/CNC quote row is only the holder subplate; metal plate is owner-made.

title: Battery stand power carrier Rev F standard-battery access
release_position: Compact steel chassis-bolted battery stand: 3 mm 340 x 265 top tray/deck around a standard N70/27-class 318 x 180 x 230 battery envelope, removable battery hold-down and lift-out path, widened 660 x 310 front/radiator-side access ladder, folded aluminium top-front cutoff base/guard, and one known 4 mm formed chassis saddle/upright bridge. The saddle drops over the chassis rail from above, has legs down both sides, and is through-bolted through both saddle legs/chassis at the one pickup location. The already-fabricated Relay Rev C folded tray mounts outside the battery footprint on the outboard/access edge with the 280 x 185 relay plastic rear guard/underlay ahead of the folded metal tray and a plain covered relay enclosure facing a removable-cover service path. Reserve upper red harness, side braided loom, and lower auxiliary relay exits. MIDI Rev C open plate/subplate mounts on a separated shallow top-front shelf with one common feed side, attached output comb/gland strip, one enlarged double-wire output hole, and five heavy output cables from the opposite side; folded cutoff base/guard sits at the top/front accessible corner with a 210 x 150 mm flat pattern, 170 x 110 mm finished face, and 20 mm lips bent upward around the 100A breaker/terminal side. Cutoff output splits into two direct protected branches: one to the covered relay feed and one to the MIDI common feed, with an 80 mm cable gutter and five-output fanout clearance. May 14 image-based starting estimate: 180 mm chassis-top-to-tray-underside rise, adjustable 150-210 mm, and 120 mm wing-side/outboard tray-centre jog from the chassis pickup centreline, adjustable 90-150 mm. The inboard engine/LHD steering side is a keep-clear/service envelope by default. Final drilling/welding remains gated by battery-installed cavity map, LHD mock-up, the one chassis saddle measurement, 100A breaker body dimensions and stud spacing, direct cable lug sweeps, battery lift-out, relay cover access, bonnet clearance, steering/radiator/fan/hose clearance, and battery terminal orientation.
notes: User update 2026-05-15 serviceability redesign: widen the access ladder to 660 x 310, use a 340 x 265 tray around a 318 x 180 x 230 standard battery envelope, keep the battery removable after hold-down removal, move the covered relay box outside the battery footprint on the outboard/access edge, preserve the flat plastic rear guard/underlay ahead of the folded metal tray, reserve three relay wire exits, separate MIDI/cutoff on top-front shelf zones, attach the MIDI output comb/gland strip with one enlarged double-wire output hole, route cutoff output directly to both relay and MIDI, and reserve an 80 mm gutter plus five-output MIDI cable fanout clearance. May 14 no-battery photo still controls provisional chassis pickup geometry: target 180 mm rise and 120 mm wing-side/outboard side-jog, with vertical/lateral adjustment retained for mock-up; chassis fixing remains a formed saddle over both rail sides, not a flat pickup plate.

title: Electrical device models Rev A
release_position: Separate S3 reference visualisation for the physical electrical devices before they are placed into the battery carrier: 300 x 197 x 80 covered relay/fuse box with plain removable cover, cover screws, upper red harness exit, side braided loom exit, lower auxiliary exit, and service loop; active five-position MIDI holder bank on the 140 x 85 insulated board with red covers, black mounting ears, one common feed side, attached output comb/gland strip, one enlarged double-wire output pass-through, and five heavy output cable side; 100A waterproof resettable breaker/cutoff with raised faceplate, red reset lever, terminal studs, ring lugs, and cable boots; hidden/security needle switch shown separately as a cabin/security reference only.
notes: Visual model only; use existing Relay Rev C, MIDI Rev C, and battery_power_carrier_mount_rev_a fabrication drawings for cut files. Exact 100A breaker body dimensions, mounting-hole centres, terminal-stud spacing, relay cover access, three relay wire exits, MIDI double-output hole, and cable-lug sweep remain measurement holds before final metal drilling.

title: Front radiator two-side retention bracket Rev A
release_position: Right-side radiator retention bracket/template pack derived from the measured existing chassis-attached radiator bracket. Current concept is one 4 mm mild-steel formed-angle saddle post: 48 mm measured main face, 410 mm upright/post height, 618 mm developed main-face template length, perpendicular return flange, full-depth 58 mm top return carried back to the far edge of the angle section, 70 mm chassis bridge allowance, 80 mm far-side saddle leg, one top screw point, and clean near/far lower saddle legs with no side ears that straddle both sides of the chassis/front-support section so a through-bolt can pass through both legs and the chassis. Final drilling/bending remains gated by right-side dry-fit, radiator ear offset, chassis width, through-bolt route, crush-tube/spacer decision, isolator stack, and fan clearance.
notes: User update 2026-05-13: use a bolt-through saddle over the chassis rather than welding the bracket to the chassis. Existing-side measurements are imported; only right-side transfer/dry-fit remains before final hole drilling.

title: Relay mount Rev C
release_position: Relay Rev C defines the already-fabricated folded relay carrier tray used by the current battery_power_carrier_mount_rev_a service-ladder layout. Treat a standalone relay-only installation as fallback only if the relay tray is deliberately split away from the battery stand.
notes: Relay carrier finished face is 320 x 220 mm with 360 x 255 mm flat pattern and bent returns; use those known dimensions raised in the front/radiator-side service-ladder field.
